title: mariscus baoulensis
intro: Mariscus baoulensis, also known as African ginger or Baoule ginger, is a plant species native to West Africa, characterized by its rhizomes and edible roots. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, and its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Mariscus baoulensis include relief from pain, fever, and digestive issues, as well as the potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. Herbal preparations of Mariscus baoulensis typically involve boiling the rhizomes and roots in water, or grinding them into a powder for use in teas, infusions, or topical applications.
